data.title: Flower Foods lowers FY25 outlook to reflect market uncertainties
data.description:
Shares of Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) are under pressure Friday morning following the release of disappointing first quarter results, which led the company to lower its guidance for FY25.

As a result of greater than expected category declines during the quarter, Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) saw a 1.4% decrease in revenue to a below consensus $1.55B. The results were impacted by a 0.3% drop tied to promotional pricing, a 2.7% decline in volume, while the Simple Mills acquisition added 1.6%. This contributed to a lower profit of $0.35 per share versus $0.38 last year and 2 cents below expectations. Adjusted EBITDA increased 1.6% to $162M, representing 10.4% of net sales, a 30-basis point increase.

To mitigate the category weakness reflected in the latest results, management is investing in innovation and looking at opportunities in faster-growing categories and adjacencies. This includes the expansion of Dave’s Killer Bread into the snacking category and new product launches like Nature’s Own Keto.

For 2025, the company concedes that the lowered guidance reflects “our first quarter performance, the challenging consumer environment, and potential for increased tariff costs.”

Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) now expects net sales to increase by 3.8% to 5.7%, within the range of $5.297B to $5.395B, down from the prior range of $5.403B to $5.487B, and less than the consensus estimate of $5.41B.

Adjusted earnings are now expected to be between $1.05 and $1.15 per share versus $1.11 to $1.24, previously, and less than $1.16 estimates.

Adjusted EBITDA is now targeted for $534M to $562M, lowered from its prior guidance of $560M to $591M.

These estimates include costs/gains associated with the Simple Mills acquisition, increased depreciation and amortization, higher net interest expense, and capital expenditures of $140M to $150M.

data.title: Value play: Constellation Brands rallies after Berkshire Hathaway doubles its stake
data.description:
Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) gained in early trading on Friday after Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) disclosed that it increased its stake in the alcohol company in Q1 to about 12 million shares from 5.6 million at the end of the year. The additional stocking up of Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) by Warren Buffett's firm gives it a stake of 6.6%.

On a broad look, Constellation Brands' (NYSE:STZ) portfolio aligns well with Buffett's investment philosophy, which often focuses on companies with strong brands, consistent earnings growth, and durable competitive advantages. The company's beer segment, in particular, has shown strong performance over the last few years.

Looking ahead, Constellation Brands (STZ) still has challenges, including potential tariffs on Mexican beer imports and shifting consumer preferences. However, analysts think Buffett's investment indicates confidence in Constellation Brands' (STZ) long-term prospects and attractive valuation.

On Wall Street, Citi analyst Filippo Falorni called Buffett's initial investment in STZ a "perfect example of value investing after the stock showed weakness mainly due to concerns about tariffs on its Mexican beer imports. Falorni and his team think the long-term STZ business remains very attractive with growth in the fastest-growing part of the beer category, favorable demographic trends with exposure to the faster-growing Hispanic population in the U.S., and large distribution opportunities for the Corona, Modelo, and Pacifico brands.

Shares of Constellation Brands (STZ) were up 3.8% in premarket trading on Friday after gaining 2.1% on Thursday.

data.title: IZEA commences modified Dutch auction tender offer to repurchase up to $8.7M of common stock
data.description:
• IZEA Worldwide (NASDAQ:IZEA) Friday said that it has commenced a modified “Dutch auction” tender offer to repurchase up to $8,700,000 of its common stock.
  • Under the terms of the Offer, IZEA is offering to purchase shares of its common stock at a price not less than $2.30 and not more than $2.80 per share.
  • The offer will commence on May 16, and expire on June 16, 2025.

data.title: BitMine Immersion to execute 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• BitMine Immersion Technologies (OTCQX:BMNR) will execute a 1 for 20 reverse split of its common stock, effective May 16, 2025.
  • Jonathan Bates, BitMine CEO, was quoted as saying, "This reverse split is part of our efforts to meet listing requirements in connection with the Company's application to list on a national securities exchange."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Microsoft in focus as Europe seeks feedback after Office-Teams offer
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) was in focus on Friday after the European Commission said it was seeking feedback for an offer that the Windows maker made an offer to address antitrust concerns.

“Under the proposed commitments, Microsoft would make available versions of these suites without Teams and at a reduced price; allow customers to switch to suites without Teams, including in the framework of existing contracts; offer Teams' competitors increased interoperability with other Microsoft products; and allow customers to move their data out of Teams to facilitate the use of competing solutions. Interested parties are invited to submit their views on Microsoft's proposed commitments,” the European watchdog said in a statement.

Shares were little changed in premarket trading.

Microsoft's commitments would remain in effect for seven years, except for its interoperability and data portability obligations, which would remain in force for 10 years, the E.C. added. 

It was reported earlier this week that Microsoft was likely to avoid a hefty penalty from the European Union after its latest offer to settle the antitrust matter.

Microsoft came under fire when Slack, now owned by Salesforce (CRM), complained in 2020 to the E.C. that Microsoft received an unfair advantage by bundling Office and Teams together.

In July 2023, the European Commission opened a formal antitrust investigation to look into the matter. Nearly a year later, in June 2024, the Commission sent a statement of objections that it believed Microsoft had abused its dominant position in the market for software-as-a-service productivity apps since at least April 2019.

data.title: Oracle, Cleveland Clinic, and G42 ink pact to launch AI-based healthcare delivery platform
data.description:
Oracle Health, Cleveland Clinic, and G42 Friday announced a strategic partnership to develop an AI-based healthcare delivery platform.

The platform will serve as the foundation for an AI-driven healthcare hub, combining Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) AI Data Platform, and Oracle Health applications, Cleveland Clinic's clinical expertise, and G42's capabilities in sovereign AI infrastructure, health data integration, and advanced clinical AI models. 

"Aging populations, rising costs, and the complexity of care demand a complete reinvention of how healthcare is provided," said Oracle Executive Chairman and CTO Larry Ellison. 

The collaboration between Cleveland Clinic, G42, and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) is part of a non-binding agreement.

data.title: RBC Bearings Non-GAAP EPS of $2.83 beats by $0.13, revenue of $437.7M misses by $2.56M
data.description:
• RBC Bearings press release (NYSE:RBC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.83 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $437.7M (+5.8% Y/Y) misses by $2.56M.
  • The Company expects net sales to be approximately $424.0 million to $434.0 million vs $427.5M consensus in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, compared to $406.3 million in the prior year, for a growth rate of 4.4% to 6.8%. Gross margin is expected to be in the range of 44.25% to 44.75% and SG&A as a percentage of net sales is expected to be in the range of 16.75% to 17.25%.

data.title: Starbucks is sized up by analysts after a sell-side meeting with the new CFO
data.description:
Analysts are weighing in on Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) after the company held a sell-side meeting with new CFO Cathy Smith in New York City.

Some of the highlights of the meeting were that Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) continues to explore options for China and is focused on margins, overhead costs, and budgeting even as it looks to improve speed of service and customer loyalty. Notably, capex spending is expected to come down this year as the company takes a harder look at build costs, renovation costs, and unit growth rate.

TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles said the firm appreciated new CFO Cathy Smith's prioritization and desire to increase transparency. "As the company invests in the Back to Starbucks plan & increases labor rosters, we expect it to focus the story on consolidated EBIT margins via G&A reduction as part of Zero Based Budgeting efforts," wrote Charles.

Morgan Stanley analyst Brian Harbour said that consistent with earnings recently, the message was clear that the Starbucks (SBUX) turnaround will take time to make the experience of going to Starbucks better. He highlighted that some metrics, like throughput and brand health, will lag financial performance and EPS.

Deutsche Bank analyst Lauren Silberman highlighted that it is clear Starbucks (SBUX) plans to increase labor hours across the system, and expects it will lead to increased transactions. She noted the company sees opportunities to unlock efficiencies across the P&L over time. "Overall, we acknowledge visibility into near-term numbers is low, though we believe SBUX is making the right investments for the business long-term, and we continue to have high conviction in the success of the turnaround and see potential upside to the run-rate earnings power and valuation," wrote Silberman.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) are down 4.4% on a year-to-date basis and are 26% below the 52-week high. Short interest on SBUX stands at 2.9% of the total float, which is above the historical average.

data.title: BioMarin to buy enzyme therapy developer Inozyme in $270M all-cash deal
data.description:
BioMarin Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:BMRN) on Friday agreed to acquire Inozyme Pharma (NASDAQ:INZY), a developer of enzyme therapies, for $4.00 per share, or a total consideration of roughly $270M in an all-cash transaction.

The director boards of both companies have approved the deal, which is expected to close in Q3 2025, subject to regulatory and other customary closing conditions. BioMarin (NASDAQ:BMRN) shares traded slightly lower after the announcement, while Inozyme (NASDAQ:INZY) shares remain on hold for trading.

In light of the agreement, San Rafael, California-based BMRN reaffirmed its previously issued 2025 financial guidance, excluding the impact from a finalized M&A transaction with Inozyme (NASDAQ:INZY).

The buyout will add Inozyme’s (INZY) late-stage enzyme replacement therapy, INZ-701, to BioMarin’s (NASDAQ:BMRN) portfolio, which comprises eight commercial therapies and other clinical and preclinical assets.

INZ-701, designed to target a rare disorder called ectonucleotide pyrophosphatase/phosphodiesterase 1 deficiency, is on track for a potential approval in 2027, with its data from a pivotal Phase 3 trial in children expected next year.

data.title: Bionoid Pharma to rebrand itself as AI Maverick Intel
data.description:
• Bionoid Pharma (OTCPK:BINP) said it will rebrand itself as AI Maverick Intel as the firm looks to use advanced artificial intelligence technologies to boost customer acquisition and engagement across healthcare, biotech, and other sectors.
  • The firm added that the rebranding comes after the acquisition of AI Maverick Intel.
